assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response clearly defines 'available' as versions officially available in the release channel that users can manually upgrade to, with specific example of using gcloud container clusters upgrade command

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — typically the same as auto-upgrade target but can differ especially during new minor version introduction (separate promotion stage)
assertions.reason: Response defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation when running gcloud container clusters create, and explains it can differ from auto-upgrade target during version rollouts with staged promotion timeline

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically — this is cluster-specific based on policies
assertions.reason: Response clearly defines auto-upgrade target as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically, emphasizes it's cluster-specific, and mentions it considers maintenance policies

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response explains that default and auto-upgrade target can differ during version rollouts, mentions staged rollout progression, and addresses the common misconception that they should always be the same

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions) and is cluster-specific — different clusters on the same channel can have different targets
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions that auto-upgrade target considers maintenance policies, maintenance exclusions, maintenance windows, and states it's cluster-specific with specific constraints affecting it

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: While the response mentions maintenance exclusions like 'no minor upgrades', it does not specifically explain that clusters with 'no minor' exclusions will have patch-only auto-upgrade targets

assertions.expectation: Recommends using gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info to check the actual auto-upgrade status, target versions, and EoS timestamps
assertions.reason: Response provides the specific gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info command with proper syntax and explains it shows auto-upgrade target, timing, and constraints

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE release schedule or release notes as resources for tracking version availability
